sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy options for prostate cancer include androgen deprivation therapy (ADT), androgen receptor pathway inhibitors (ARPIs), chemotherapy, immunotherapy, PARP inhibitors, radiopharmaceuticals, and targeted agents. ADT forms the backbone of treatment for metastatic and recurrent disease. Treatment intensification with ARPIs and/or docetaxel is strongly recommended for mCSPC. For mCRPC, treatment options are organized by prior ARPI and docetaxel exposure. Biomarker-directed therapy (PARP inhibitors for HRR mutations, pembrolizumab for MSI-H/dMMR) and radiopharmaceuticals (Lu-177-PSMA-617, radium-223) have expanded the therapeutic landscape. FDA-approved biosimilar agents are appropriate substitutes for recommended biologic therapies [PROS-N].

cards.name: Neoadjuvant/Concurrent/Adjuvant ADT with RT for Localized Disease
cards.drugs: LHRH agonist or antagonist, first-generation antiandrogen, abiraterone
cards.detail: LHRH agonist or antagonist monotherapy or with first-generation antiandrogen; for very-high-risk or N1: plus abiraterone (2 years) [PROS-G]. Rationale: ST-ADT (4-6 months) for UIR; LT-ADT (18-36 months) for high/very-high risk. Concurrent/adjuvant ADT preferred over neoadjuvant [PROS-G]. Alternatives: Orchiectomy for castration. Biomarker requirements: None.

cards.name: M0 CSPC (Regional N1, M0; First M0 RP or RT recurrence)
cards.drugs: Orchiectomy, LHRH agonist, LHRH antagonist, first-generation antiandrogen, abiraterone
cards.detail: Orchiectomy, LHRH agonist, or LHRH antagonist ± first-generation antiandrogen; for N1: plus abiraterone [PROS-G]. Rationale: ADT for regional disease or M0 recurrent disease. Alternatives: ADT with abiraterone for N1 only [PROS-G]. Biomarker requirements: None.

cards.name: M0 CSPC BCR2
cards.detail: Monitoring (preferred for low-risk BCR2); ADT for high-risk BCR2; enzalutamide ± leuprolide or apalutamide + ADT for high-risk BCR2 [PROS-12]. Rationale: Monitoring preferred for low-risk BCR2; treatment intensification for high-risk BCR2 based on EMBARK and PRESTO trials. Alternatives: ADT monotherapy; intermittent ADT. Biomarker requirements: PSADT and risk stratification.

cards.name: mCSPC - Low Volume (Synchronous or Metachronous)
cards.drugs: ADT, abiraterone, apalutamide, enzalutamide, darolutamide, docetaxel, niraparib, abiraterone
cards.detail: ADT + abiraterone, apalutamide, or enzalutamide (category 1); or ADT + darolutamide (category 2B) [PROS-14]. Rationale: Doublet therapy with ADT + ARPI; triplet therapy with ADT + docetaxel + ARPI for fit patients (category 2B); EBRT to primary tumor for synchronous low-volume [PROS-14]. Alternatives: Triplet therapy; ADT + niraparib/abiraterone for BRCA2m; ADT + EBRT to primary ± ARPI or docetaxel. Biomarker requirements: BRCA2 mutation for niraparib/abiraterone.

cards.name: mCSPC - High Volume
cards.drugs: ADT, docetaxel, abiraterone, darolutamide, ARPI, niraparib, abiraterone
cards.detail: ADT + docetaxel + abiraterone (category 1) or ADT + docetaxel + darolutamide (category 1) [PROS-15]. Rationale: Triplet therapy preferred based on PEACE-1 and ARASENS; doublet therapy also appropriate. Alternatives: ADT + ARPI doublet; ADT + niraparib/abiraterone for BRCA2m. Biomarker requirements: BRCA2 mutation for niraparib/abiraterone.

cards.name: M0 CRPC
cards.detail: Monitoring for PSADT >10 months (preferred); Apalutamide (cat1), Darolutamide (cat1), or Enzalutamide (cat1) for PSADT ≤10 months [PROS-16]. Rationale: Secondary hormone therapy to delay metastases. Alternatives: Other secondary hormone therapies (abiraterone, enzalutamide following ARPI). Biomarker requirements: PSADT assessment; document castrate testosterone levels.

cards.name: M1 CRPC - Pre-ARPI
cards.drugs: Abiraterone, enzalutamide, docetaxel, PARP inhibitor combinations
cards.detail: Abiraterone (cat1) or Enzalutamide (cat1) [PROS-18]. Rationale: First-line ARPI for mCRPC without prior ARPI exposure. Alternatives: Docetaxel (cat1); PARP inhibitor combinations for BRCAm/HRRm. Biomarker requirements: HRR gene mutations for PARP inhibitors; MSI-H/dMMR for pembrolizumab; TMB testing; PSMA-PET for Lu-177-PSMA-617 eligibility.

cards.name: M1 CRPC - Post-ARPI/Pre-Docetaxel
cards.drugs: Docetaxel, PARP inhibitors (olaparib, rucaparib), Lu-177-PSMA-617, cabazitaxel, carboplatin
cards.detail: Docetaxel (cat1) [PROS-18]. Rationale: Second-line after ARPI progression. Alternatives: PARP inhibitors (olaparib/cat1, rucaparib/cat1 preferred for BRCAm); Lu-177-PSMA-617 for PSMA-positive; cabazitaxel/carboplatin for aggressive variant. Biomarker requirements: BRCA/HRR mutations; PSMA status; aggressive variant criteria.

cards.name: M1 CRPC - Post-ARPI/Post-Docetaxel
cards.drugs: Cabazitaxel, docetaxel, PARP inhibitors (olaparib), Lu-177-PSMA-617, cabazitaxel/carboplatin, mitoxantrone
cards.detail: Cabazitaxel (cat1) or Docetaxel rechallenge [PROS-18]. Rationale: After progression on both ARPI and docetaxel. Alternatives: PARP inhibitors (olaparib/cat1 for BRCAm); Lu-177-PSMA-617 (cat1 for PSMA-positive); cabazitaxel/carboplatin; mitoxantrone for palliation. Biomarker requirements: BRCA/HRR mutations; PSMA status.

cards.name: M1 CRPC - Additional Options (Any Prior Therapy)
cards.detail: Sipuleucel-T for asymptomatic without visceral metastases; Pembrolizumab for MSI-H/dMMR (cat2B); MDT for oligometastatic/oligoprogressive; Radium-223 for symptomatic bone metastases. Biomarker requirements: MSI-H/dMMR for pembrolizumab; PSMA status; symptomatic bone metastases.

timeline.title: 1st Line
timeline.body: Metastatic hormone-sensitive (mCSPC): ADT + ARPI (abiraterone, apalutamide, enzalutamide, darolutamide) with/without docetaxel. Also ADT + niraparib/abiraterone for BRCA2m.

timeline.title: 2nd Line
timeline.body: Non-metastatic castration-resistant (M0 CRPC): Apalutamide, darolutamide, or enzalutamide for PSADT ≤10 months; monitoring for PSADT >10 months.

timeline.title: 3rd Line
timeline.body: Metastatic castration-resistant pre-ARPI (mCRPC): Abiraterone or enzalutamide; docetaxel alternative; PARP inhibitors for HRRm.

timeline.title: 4th Line
timeline.body: Metastatic castration-resistant post-ARPI (mCRPC): Docetaxel; cabazitaxel; PARP inhibitors; Lu-177-PSMA-617; radium-223; others depending on biomarker and prior therapy.
